data.title: 3M in charts: Q1 revenue grew Y/Y across all business segments
Plus Therapeutics regains compliance with Nasdaq
Blue Moon Metals, Alpha Future Funds to combine holdings in Norway's Sulitjelma mining district
data.description:
Blue Moon Metals (BMM) up 2.9% pre-market Tuesday after saying it entered into a non-binding letter of intent with Alpha Future Funds to combine wholly-owned subsidiaries Nye Sulitjelma Gruver and VMS Explorations into a single entity to combine holdings in Norway's Sulitjelma mining district.

The Sulitjelma mine produced over 470 kt of copper and 120K oz of gold over a 100-year life until it was shut in 1991 and benefits from excellent existing surface and underground infrastructure ready to be refurbished, including connection to abundant clean hydropower; an exploration campaign undertaken last year by VMS made two new copper-gold discoveries and identified a further 18 drill targets.

Blue Moon (BMM) and Alpha Future Funds said they are committed to restarting the historic Sulitjelma mining district and re-establish Norway as a hub for metals and mining in Europe.

data.title: D.R. Horton stock rises as FQ2 earnings beat despite cautious homebuyer sentiment
data.description:
D.R. Horton (DHI) stock was rising as fiscal second-quarter earnings exceeded expectations despite affordability constraints and cautious consumer sentiment continuing to impact new home demand.

Shares were 2.39% higher at $157.00 during pre-market trading on Tuesday.

The Arlington, Texas-based homebuilder posted a GAAP EPS of $2.24, which beats the average analyst estimate by $0.10. Revenue of $7.6B (-2.2% Y/Y) was in line with the consensus.

During the quarter, homebuilding revenue decreased 2% on a yearly basis to $7.1B, while homes closed increased 1% to 19,486 homes.

For the non-homebuilding segments, revenue from rental operations came in at $211.8M from the sale of 566 single-family rental homes and 216 multi-family rental units. Forestar sold 2,938 lots and generated $374.3M of revenue. Financial services revenue totaled $192.8M.

Consolidated pre-tax income totaled $867.4M, and pre-tax profit margin came to 11.5%, benefiting from a favorable litigation outcome and lower warranty costs.

"Our tenured operators executed with discipline, driving an 11% year‑over‑year increase in net sales orders, while reducing unsold completed homes by 35% from a year ago," said Executive Chairman David Auld.

"We expect our sales incentives to remain elevated in fiscal 2026, with incentive levels dependent on demand, mortgage interest rates, and other market conditions," said Auld.

The company reiterated its fiscal 2026 guidance of an income tax rate of ~24.5%, consolidated cash flow provided by operations of at least $3B, share repurchases of ~$2.5B, and dividend payments of ~$500M.

For the fiscal first half of 2026, D.R. Horton estimates revenue in the range of $33.5B to $34.5B. Homes closed by homebuilding operations are estimated to be 86,000 to 87,500.

The company plans to provide its fiscal third-quarter financial guidance at the earnings conference call scheduled for 8:30 AM ET today.

data.title: Getinge AB Non-GAAP EPS of SEK 1.80; maintains FY26 forecast of organic sales growth
data.description:
• Getinge AB (publ) press release (GNGBF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of SEK 1.80.
  • Net sales increased organically by 0.8% (6.2) and the order intake rose by 3.9% organically (2.9).
  • Adjusted EBITA was SEK 824 M (1,003) and the margin 11.1% (12.1).
  • Free cash flow amounted to SEK 842 M.
  • "There is currently high geopolitical uncertainty, however, based on dialogue with its customers Getinge is maintaining the forecast of organic sales growth of 3–5% in 2026."

data.title: Upbound teams with Amazon for in-store pickup, return services
data.description:
Upbound Group (UPBD) said its Rent-A-Center unit has entered an agreement with Amazon to offer in-store pickup and return services across more than 1,700 U.S. stores.

The service—expected to roll out by June 2026—will allow customers to ship orders directly to nearby Rent-A-Center stores for pickup and drop off eligible returns through a label-free, box-free process, reducing friction in last-mile logistics.

“We’re extremely proud to be among Amazon’s largest U.S. retail collaborations for pickups and returns through this agreement and welcome Amazon customers to enjoy the Rent-A-Center experience,” said Upbound CEO Fahmi Karam.

Upbound, which operates brands including Rent-A-Center, Acima, and Brigit, expands beyond its core lease-to-own model by integrating services that align with omnichannel retail trends.

UPBD stock traded 2.3% higher on Tuesday during pre-market trade.

data.title: United Community Banks Non-GAAP EPS of $0.70 in-line, revenue of $276.5M beats by $2.63M
data.description:
• United Community Banks press release (UCB): Non-GAAP EPS of $0.70 in-line.
  • Revenue of $276.5M (+6.2% Y/Y) beats by $2.63M.
  • Net interest margin of 3.65% increased by 29 basis points from a year ago and 3 basis points from the fourth quarter on a lower cost of funds and improving asset mix
  • Provision for credit losses was $10.9 million, down $4.6 million from a year ago and $2.8 million from the fourth quarter; allowance for credit losses coverage down slightly to 1.15% of total loans; net charge-offs were $10.4 million, or 0.22% of average loans, annualized
  • Customer deposits were up $237 million from the fourth quarter

data.title: Simply Good Foods to cut about 15% of staff
data.description:
The Simply Good Foods Company (SMPL) announced plans to cut approximately 15% of its staff as part of cost savings and realignment initiatives aimed at streamlining the organization.

These cost actions are expected to generate around $17M in annual savings. These actions are included in the company’s most recent outlook.

In connection with the realignment initiatives, effective immediately, the company is also implementing changes within its executive leadership team.

Chief Human Resources Officer Amy Held will step down from her role as responsibilities are consolidated under this simplified leadership structure and will depart from the company by June 1, 2026, following a transition period.

Jason Bendure, the current chief supply chain officer, will report to CEO Joe Scalzo on a range of strategic projects over the next several months and then will separate from the company.

data.title: Alaska Air points to strong underlying demand despite fuel and weather challenges
data.description:
Alaska Air Group (ALK) noted that its Q1 results were pressured by sharply higher fuel costs, as well as disruptions from weather in Hawaii and unrest in Puerto Vallarta. However, underlying demand trends remained solid during the quarter. 

The airline company reported revenue was up 5.1% to $3.3B and a GAAP net loss of $193 million, or $1.69 per share. Operationally, Alaska Air (ALK) highlighted strong execution and commercial momentum. It said it led the industry in on-time performance, completed more than 90% of its Boeing 737 cabin retrofits, and continued rolling out Starlink and other premium product upgrades. Premium revenue rose 8% year over year, managed corporate revenue increased 19%, and Seattle-Tokyo reached profitability less than a year after launch, with load factors above 90% on both Seattle-Tokyo and Seattle-Seoul.

The loyalty and partnership story was also noted to be a bright spot. Atmos Rewards membership and co-brand card remuneration both grew double digits, and the company extended its Bank of America partnership to improve economics and support further growth in 2026 and beyond.

Notably, Alaska Air (ALK) suspended full-year guidance due to the uncertainty around higher fuel costs.

Looking ahead, Bank of America said it sees current trading levels on ALK as attractive given the long-term potential from the Hawaiian assets, a recovery in key markets like Hawaii, and a normalizing cost structure. Barclays analyst Brandon Oglenski said Alaska's (ALK) Q1 results and Q2 outlook will likely face some scrutiny from investors based on higher non-fuel cost inflation and some apparently transitory Hawaii demand challenges.

Shares of Alaska Air (ALK) were down 1.6% in premarket trading.

data.title: Docebo boosts full-year revenue guidance on AI platform demand
data.description:
Docebo (DCBO) on Tuesday lifted its full-year revenue outlook, citing demand for its AI workforce training platform.

The company now sees revenue between $271M and $273M, up from prior guidance of $267.5M to $269.5M. Subscription revenue is now forecast at $253.5M to $255.5M, compared with previous guidance of $251.5M to $253.5M.

Analysts expect full-year revenue of $268.60M. Full-year adjusted EBITDA is seen between $54.5M and $56.5M, compared to the prior estimate of $52.5M to $54.5M.

The company said preliminary unaudited revenue for the quarter ended Mar. 31 is expected to be between $65.4M and $65.6M, up 14.3% from $57.3M a year earlier. Analysts are expecting it to be about $63.6M.

Docebo's largest OEM customer is expected to account for 3.2% of annual recurring revenue, down from 9.4% a year earlier. ARR, which is a key software metric, is expected to reach $248.9M as of March 31, up 10.6% from a year earlier. Docebo said foreign exchange movements reduced ARR by $1.4M during the quarter.

“Docebo delivered an exceptional start to 2026, exceeding our expectations across the board and reinforcing the strength of our market positioning. Our performance reflects both disciplined execution and growing demand for our AI workforce readiness platform as organizations prioritize workforce readiness in an increasingly complex operating environment,” said CEO Alessio Artuffo.

The company will report full first-quarter results before markets open on May 8, 2026.

Shares are up around +2.6% in premarket trading on Tuesday.
